heading: Limitation on liability for teachers

children.text: the teacher was acting within the scope of the teacher’s employment or responsibilities to a school or governmental entity;

the actions of the teacher were carried out in conformity with Federal, State, and local laws (including rules and regulations) in furtherance of efforts to control, discipline, expel, or suspend a student or maintain order or control in the classroom or school;

if appropriate or required, the teacher was properly licensed, certified, or authorized by the appropriate authorities for the activities or practice involved in the State in which the harm occurred, where the activities were or practice was undertaken within the scope of the teacher’s responsibilities;

the harm was not caused by willful or criminal misconduct, gross negligence, reckless misconduct, or a conscious, flagrant indifference to the rights or safety of the individual harmed by the teacher; and

children.heading: Exceptions to teacher liability protection
children.label: b Exceptions to teacher liability protection

children.text: A State law that requires a school or governmental entity to adhere to risk management procedures, including mandatory training of teachers.
A State law that makes the school or governmental entity liable for the acts or omissions of its teachers to the same extent as an employer is liable for the acts or omissions of its employees.
A State law that makes a limitation of liability inapplicable if the civil action was brought by an officer of a State or local government pursuant to State or local law.

children.heading: Limitation on punitive damages based on the actions of teachers
children.label: c Limitation on punitive damages based on the actions of teachers

children.heading: General rule
children.label: 1 General rule
children.text: Punitive damages may not be awarded against a teacher in an action brought for harm based on the act or omission of a teacher acting within the scope of the teacher’s employment or responsibilities to a school or governmental entity unless the claimant establishes by clear and convincing evidence that the harm was proximately caused by an act or omission of such teacher that constitutes willful or criminal misconduct, or a conscious, flagrant indifference to the rights or safety of the individual harmed.

children.heading: Construction
children.label: 2 Construction
children.text: Paragraph (1) does not create a cause of action for punitive damages and does not preempt or supersede any Federal or State law to the extent that such law would further limit the award of punitive damages.

children.heading: Exceptions to limitations on liability
children.label: d Exceptions to limitations on liability
A
children.text: constitutes a crime of violence (as that term is defined in ) or act of international terrorism (as that term is defined in ) for which the defendant has been convicted in any court;section 16 of title 18section 2331 of title 18

children.label: B
children.text: involves a sexual offense, as defined by applicable State law, for which the defendant has been convicted in any court;

children.label: C
children.text: involves misconduct for which the defendant has been found to have violated a Federal or State civil rights law; or

children.label: D
children.text: where the defendant was under the influence (as determined pursuant to applicable State law) of intoxicating alcohol or any drug at the time of the misconduct.

children.heading: Hiring
children.label: 2 Hiring
children.text: The limitations on the liability of a teacher under this subpart shall not apply to misconduct during background investigations, or during other actions, involved in the hiring of a teacher.

children.heading: Concerning responsibility of teachers to schools and governmental entities
children.label: 1 Concerning responsibility of teachers to schools and governmental entities
children.text: Nothing in this section shall be construed to affect any civil action brought by any school or any governmental entity against any teacher of such school.

children.heading: Concerning corporal punishment
children.label: 2 Concerning corporal punishment
children.text: Nothing in this subpart shall be construed to affect any State or local law (including a rule or regulation) or policy pertaining to the use of corporal punishment.
